results.possibleIdentity: Daniel Gordon was an engineer or scientist associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded in 1966 to promote collaborations between artists and the technical community. Based on historical records and technical service directories from the late 1960s, Gordon was a registered member of the E.A.T. Technical Services program, which matched artists with engineers and scientists. His professional information from this period identifies his primary technical field as crystallography and his location as the Bronx, New York. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, many technical professionals from institutions such as Bell Telephone Laboratories and IBM, as well as local universities like NYU (which then had a campus in the Bronx) and Fordham University, participated in E.A.T.'s matching service. Gordon's specific registration as a crystallographer in the Bronx suggests he may have been a researcher or academic professional in the New York scientific community who offered his expertise to assist artists with projects involving structural analysis or molecular modeling.
